hook.text: Your app has 20 microservices. Does the mobile app need to know all 20 addresses? An API gateway says no.

buildup.text: An API gateway is a single entry point that routes requests to the right service behind the scenes.

discovery.text: It also handles authentication, rate limiting, and caching — so individual services don't have to.

twist.text: The gateway becomes a single point of failure. If it goes down, nothing behind it is reachable.

climax.text: Cloud-managed gateways like AWS API Gateway and Azure API Management reduce that risk with built-in redundancy.

punchline.text: One door in. Many services behind. That's the gateway.

quiz.question: What is the role of an API gateway in microservices?
options: A single entry point that routes requests and handles cross-cutting concerns
A database that stores all API documentation
A tool that automatically generates microservice code
